mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in over 65 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST yields negative predictive power or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over 5-year out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing output that increases corporate profit margins, generating positive short-term equity returns during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative or insignificant when INDPRO trends upward during late-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index surpassing 240 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in 70 percent of months following CPIAUCSL breaches above 240, or signal loses predictive power after 2020.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that sustains consumer spending and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC for PAYEMS changes falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or forward returns show no positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and rising personal consumption expenditures signals unsustainable demand that precedes earnings revisions and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of cases when the divergence condition is met, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and declining federal funds rates creates a favorable supply-side environment that boosts corporate output and generates positive short-term returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns with statistical significance, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 during easing periods.
